channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Court ruling freezes Paramount-Warner Bros. merger for now
item.description: Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.
item.content:encoded:
Paramount's owners were hoping they'd be firmly in control of rival Warner Bros. Discovery by the end of this week. Monday's temporary restraining order puts that plan on hold.

(Image credit: SOPA Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
item.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.content:encoded: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: China signals possible return of U.S. trade privileges for Hong Kong
item.description: The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.
item.content:encoded:
The decision comes two months after President Donald Trump met with Xi Jinping, potentially warming ties ahead of Xi's expected U.S. visit.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The new $1 Trump coin doesn't just buck norms. Experts say it also breaks laws
item.description: President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's face will appear on a new commemorative coin honoring the nation's 250th birthday. It's one of the many unusual places his likeness has popped up this year.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Federal Reserve looks for 'secret sauce' behind a successful Wisconsin toolmaker
item.description: Business is booming for Snap-on, a Wisconsin company that has made tools for professional mechanics for more than a century. It recently got a fact-finding visit from the head of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank.
item.content:encoded:
Business is booming for Snap-on, a Wisconsin company that has made tools for professional mechanics for more than a century. It recently got a fact-finding visit from the head of the Chicago Federal Reserve Bank.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Kalshi says it's not a sportsbook even as World Cup bets surge
item.description: The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.
item.content:encoded:
The betting site Kalshi emerged as a dominant sports betting platform during the World Cup. But the company avoids billions of dollars in taxes by insisting it is not a sports gambling operator.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley/AP Photo/Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
item.description: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: American AI is expensive. Some startups are turning to cheap Chinese models
item.description: AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.
item.content:encoded:
AI is a fast-growing business expense. Some companies are cutting costs by switching to cheaper Chinese AI models.

(Image credit: Imen Ben Youssef/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Mystery bidder buys T. rex nicknamed 'Gus' for a record $50 million
item.description: A T-rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder. But many aren't happy about it.
item.content:encoded:
A T-rex fossil billed as one of the world's largest specimens was sold for a record $50.1 million Tuesday to a mystery bidder. But many aren't happy about it.

(Image credit: Matthew Sherman/Sotheby's via AP)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: Inflation slowed sharply -- but it may not last
item.description: Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.
item.content:encoded:
Annual inflation hit 3.5% in June, down from May's more than three-year high — but the resumption of the conflict with Iran threaten to push up inflation as energy costs once again spike.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha
